assumptions.read: Footprint is a CTA-local accumulator upper-bound planner, not a runtime benchmark. It asks whether a D-block tile can hold unique compressed-group accumulators while reducing dKV before global writes. The default assumes online softmax row statistics rather than a materialized score tile. The streaming microtile budget separately adds QK and grad-prob scratch for the selected-key loop.

tile_pressure.read: Pressure estimates are shape-level heuristics. They measure how much selected-key loop work a persistent tile has to cover before it can reduce dKV writes, not a measured runtime.
Pressure estimates are shape-level heuristics. They measure how much selected-key loop work a persistent tile has to cover before it can reduce dKV writes, not a measured runtime.

decision_read: If only low-reduction small tiles fit the LDS-style budget, tile-local coalescing is not enough by itself. If high-reduction wide tiles fit only for narrow D blocks, the next kernel should be a streaming MFMA/TileLang/CK schedule that processes D in blocks and fuses LI/compressor gradient production around the query-major selected-key loop. The pressure-balanced list keeps only fitting candidates whose selected-key microtile loop is at most 256 microsteps per D-block tile; it is a first-kernel implementability filter, not a proof of optimality.
